name: Peking Duck 1
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean a fresh duck thoroughly and pump it full of air through the neck to separate the skin from the meat using a bicycle pump. Pour boiling water over the duck three times. Carefully dry the duck, slit the stomach, and remove the innards.
2. Prepare the marinade by mixing hot water, maltose, and vinegar. Rub the mixture all over the outside of the duck.
3. Hang the duck by its neck at room temperature (around 65°F) for at least 12 hours to marinate.
4. The next day, preheat the oven to 400°F. Place the duck in a pan and cook for 10 minutes. Turn the heat up to 450°F and continue cooking for an additional 30 minutes or until the meat is tender and the skin is crispy.
5. To carve the duck, place it breast side up and cut downwards towards the head. Slice thinly using only the outer slices that have skin. Slice both breasts and the legs, cutting from the joint to the end of each leg. Discard any remaining meat without skin or use it for another dish.
